DIA_GEU.name: Ectopic Pregnancy
DIA_GEU.description: Development of a fertilized egg elsewhere than in the uterus (as in a fallopian tube or the peritoneal cavity).

DIA_SOPK.name: PCOS
DIA_SOPK.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome or polycystic ovarian syndrome. A variable disease that is marked by amenorrhea, hirsutism, obesity, infertility, and ovarian enlargement and is usually initiated by an elevated level of luteinizing hormone, androgen, or estrogen which results in an abnormal cycle of gonadotropin release by the pituitary gland.

DIA_SD_PREMENSTRUEL.name: Premenstrual Syndrome
DIA_SD_PREMENSTRUEL.description: A varying group of symptoms manifested by some women prior to menstruation.

DIA_MENOPAUSE.name: Menopause
DIA_MENOPAUSE.description: The natural cessation of menstruation that usually occurs between the ages of 45 and 55.

DIA_FIBROME_SM.name: Submucous Fibroma
DIA_FIBROME_SM.description: A benign tumor consisting mainly of fibrous tissue.

DIA_FIBROME_I.name: Interstitial Fibroma
DIA_FIBROME_I.description: A benign tumor consisting mainly of fibrous tissue.

DIA_FIBROME_SS.name: Subserous Fibroma
DIA_FIBROME_SS.description: A benign tumor consisting mainly of fibrous tissue.

DIA_KYSTE_OVARIEN1.name: Organic Ovarian Cyst
DIA_KYSTE_OVARIEN1.description: An ovarian cyst is a fluid-filled sac within the ovary. Organic cysts can complicate or degenerate.

DIA_KYSTE_OVARIEN2.name: Functional Ovarian Cyst
DIA_KYSTE_OVARIEN2.description: An ovarian cyst is a fluid-filled sac within the ovary. Functional cysts grow during the menstrual cycle due to a hormonal imbalance.

DIA_TORSION_KYSTE_OVARIEN.name: Ovarian Torsion
DIA_TORSION_KYSTE_OVARIEN.description: Ovarian torsion is when an ovary twists on its attachment to other structures.

DIA_ABCES_OVARIEN.name: Ovarian Abscess
DIA_ABCES_OVARIEN.description: An ovarian abscess is a pus-filled pocket in an ovary. Usually caused by bacteria.

DIA_PROLAPSUS_GENITAL.name: Prolapse
DIA_PROLAPSUS_GENITAL.description: The falling down or slipping of a body part from its usual position or relations.

DIA_GONOCOCCIE.name: Gonorrhea
DIA_GONOCOCCIE.description: A contagious inflammation of the genital mucous membrane caused by the gonococcus.

DIA_CHLAMYDIOSE.name: Chlamydia
DIA_CHLAMYDIOSE.description: A disease or infection caused by chlamydiae.

DIA_TRICHOMONOSE.name: Trichomoniasis
DIA_TRICHOMONOSE.description: Infection with or disease caused by trichomonads.

DIA_MYCOSE.name: Vaginal Mycosis
DIA_MYCOSE.description: Infection with or disease caused by a fungus.

DIA_IGE.name: Pelvic Inflammatory Disease
DIA_IGE.description: Inflammation of the female reproductive tract (such as the fallopian tubes and ovaries) that occurs especially as a result of a sexually transmitted disease and is a leading cause of infertility in women.

DIA_ENDOMETRIOSE.name: Endometriosis
DIA_ENDOMETRIOSE.description: The presence and growth of functioning endometrial tissue in places other than the uterus.

DIA_COL_UTERUS_BENIN.name: Precancerous Cervix
DIA_COL_UTERUS_BENIN.description: Precancerous conditions of the cervix are changes to cervical cells that make them more likely to develop into cancer.

DIA_POLYPE_COL.name: Cervical Polyp
DIA_POLYPE_COL.description: A growth projecting from a mucous membrane.

DIA_K_COL_UTERUS.name: Cervical Cancer
DIA_K_COL_UTERUS.description: Cervical cancer is a cancer arising from the cervix. It is due to the abnormal growth of cells that have the ability to invade or spread to other parts of the body.

DIA_K_CORPS_UTERUS.name: Uterus Cancer
DIA_K_CORPS_UTERUS.description: Cancer of the womb (uterine or endometrial cancer) is a common cancer that affects the female reproductive system. It's more common in women who have been through the menopause.

DIA_K_OVAIRE.name: Ovarian Cancer
DIA_K_OVAIRE.description: Ovarian cancer is a type of cancer that begins in the ovaries.

DIA_ADENOFIBROME.name: Fibroadenoma
DIA_ADENOFIBROME.description: A fibroadenoma is a type of lump that can develop in the breast and typically appears in females. Fibroadenomas are very common, but they are benign, which means that they are noncancerous.

DIA_MFK.name: Fibrocystic Breast
DIA_MFK.description: Fibrocystic breast disease, commonly called fibrocystic breasts or fibrocystic change, is a benign (noncancerous) condition in which the breasts feel lumpy. Fibrocystic breasts aren't harmful or dangerous, but may be bothersome or uncomfortable for some women.

DIA_K_SEIN.name: Breast Cancer
DIA_K_SEIN.description: Breast cancer is a disease in which cells in the breast grow out of control. Breast cancer can begin in different parts of the breast.

DIA_HTA_G.name: Pregnancy-Induced Hypertension
DIA_HTA_G.description: Gestational Hypertension also referred to as Pregnancy-Induced Hypertension (PIH) is a condition characterized by high blood pressure during pregnancy. Gestational Hypertension can lead to a serious condition called Preeclampsia.

DIA_DG.name: Gestational Diabetes Mellitus
DIA_DG.description: Gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) is defined as any degree of glucose intolerance with onset or first recognition during pregnancy.

DIA_CHORIOAMNIOTITE.name: Chorioamnionitis
DIA_CHORIOAMNIOTITE.description: Chorioamnionitis is a bacterial infection that occurs before or during labor. The name refers to the membranes surrounding the fetus: the “chorion” (outer membrane) and the “amnion” (fluid-filled sac).

DIA_CA_G.name: Pregnancy Cystitis
DIA_CA_G.description: Cystitis is an infection of the bladder that causes a burning sensation when peeing.

DIA_PNA_G.name: Pyelonephritis
DIA_PNA_G.description: Pyelonephritis is inflammation of the kidney, usually due to ascending bacteria spreading up through the urinary tract.

DIA_VAGINOSE.name: Pregnancy Vaginosis
DIA_VAGINOSE.description: A bacterial infection of the vagina causing a smelly white discharge.

DIA_BABY_BLUES.name: Baby Blues
DIA_BABY_BLUES.description: A feeling of sadness that is experienced by a woman shortly after giving birth, usually lasts less than two weeks. Baby blues isn't an illness or even a proper psychiatric diagnosis, it is simply a way that people have tried to describe unpleasant feelings that occur shortly after having a baby and tend to resolve in a few days.

DIA_DEPRESSION_PP.name: Postpartum Depression
DIA_DEPRESSION_PP.description: Postpartum depression is a mood disorder that can affect women after childbirth. Mothers with postpartum depression experience feelings of extreme sadness, anxiety, and exhaustion that may make it difficult for them to complete daily care activities for themselves or for others.

DIA_EA_PP.name: Postpartum Endometritis
DIA_EA_PP.description: Postpartum endometritis refers to infection of the decidua.

DIA_EM_PP.name: Breast Engorgement
DIA_EM_PP.description: Breast engorgement means breasts are painfully overfull of milk. This usually occurs when a mother makes more milk than her baby uses.

DIA_CM_PP.name: Cracked Nipple
DIA_CM_PP.description: Cracked nipple (or nipple trauma) is a condition that can occur in breastfeeding women as a result of a number of possible causes. Developing a cracked nipple can result in soreness, dryness or irritation to, or bleeding of, one or both nipples during breastfeeding.

DIA_LM_PP.name: Mastitis
DIA_LM_PP.description: Mastitis is an inflammation of breast tissue that sometimes involves an infection. The inflammation results in breast pain, swelling, warmth and redness.

DIA_GALACTOPHORITE.name: Infectious Mastitis
DIA_GALACTOPHORITE.description: Inflammation of the mammary gland in the breast or udder, typically due to bacterial infection.

DIA_ABCES_MAMMAIRE.name: Breast Abscess
DIA_ABCES_MAMMAIRE.description: A breast abscess is a painful collection of pus that forms in the breast. Most abscesses develop just under the skin and are caused by a bacterial infection.

DIA_TVP_PP.name: Postpartum Deep Vein Thrombosis
DIA_TVP_PP.description: Deep vein thrombosis, or DVT, is the development of a blood clot in a deep vein.

DIA_TVPELV.name: Pelvic Vein Thrombosis
DIA_TVPELV.description: Pelvic vein thrombosis may include ovarian vein thrombosis, deep pelvic vein thrombosis, septic pelvic thrombophlebitis.

DIA_AMENORHEE_HF.name: Functional Hypothalamic Amenorrhea
DIA_AMENORHEE_HF.description: Functional hypothalamic amenorrhea (FHA) is defined as a non-organic and reversible disorder in which the impairment of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) pulsatile secretion plays a key role.

DIA_MOLE_HYDATIFORME.name: Molar Pregnancy
DIA_MOLE_HYDATIFORME.description: Molar pregnancy is an abnormal form of pregnancy in which a non-viable fertilized egg implants in the uterus and will fail to come to term. A molar pregnancy is a gestational trophoblastic disease[1] which grows into a mass in the uterus that has swollen chorionic villi.

DIA_NECROBIOSE_ASEPTIQUE.name: Aseptic Necrobiosis
DIA_NECROBIOSE_ASEPTIQUE.description: Aseptic necrobiosis (red degeneration) of a uterine fibroid is defined as a hemorrhagic infarction of a previously hyalinized myoma caused by ischaemic necrosis as the rapid fibroid growth outweighs its blood supply.

DIA_ADENOMYOSE.name: Adenomyosis
DIA_ADENOMYOSE.description: Adenomyosis is a gynecologic medical condition characterized by the abnormal presence of endometrial tissue (the inner lining of the uterus) within the myometrium (the thick, muscular layer of the uterus).

DIA_SD_SHEEHAN.name: Sheehan's Syndrome
DIA_SD_SHEEHAN.description: Sheehan's syndrome, also known as postpartum pituitary gland necrosis, is hypopituitarism (decreased functioning of the pituitary gland), caused by ischemic necrosis due to blood loss and hypovolemic shock during and after childbirth.

DIA_CHOC_TOXIQUE_SD.name: Toxic Shock Syndrome
DIA_CHOC_TOXIQUE_SD.description: Toxic shock syndrome is a condition caused by bacterial toxins.

DIA_OBGYN_IU_EFFORT.name: Stress Incontinence
DIA_OBGYN_IU_EFFORT.description: Stress incontinence corresponds to an involuntary loss of effort (laughter, coughing, sneezing, sport ...), without feeling of need.

DIA_OBGYN_IU_HYPERACTIVITE_VESICALE.name: Overactive Bladder
DIA_OBGYN_IU_HYPERACTIVITE_VESICALE.description: Overactive bladder corresponds to involuntary loss of urine, preceded by an immediately urgent and uninhibited need (urgency or urgent need), which can occur at rest, at night, without any notion of effort.

DIA_OBGYN_SALPINGITE.name: Salpingitis
DIA_OBGYN_SALPINGITE.description: Salpingitis is an infection and inflammation in the Fallopian tubes.

DIA_OBGYN_ENDOMETRITE.name: Endometritis
DIA_OBGYN_ENDOMETRITE.description: Endometritis is inflammation of the inner lining of the uterus.

DIA_OBGYN_SYPHILIS_I.name: Syphilis (I)
DIA_OBGYN_SYPHILIS_I.description: Primary syphilis is marked, after about 3 weeks of silent incubation, by the appearance of the chancre at the point of inoculation of the treponema and its lymphadenopathy.

DIA_OBGYN_SYPHILIS_II.name: Syphilis (II)
DIA_OBGYN_SYPHILIS_II.description: Secondary syphilis is marked by several mucocutaneous rashes interspersed with asymptomatic phases of a few weeks or months and last two years on average. General and visceral signs are associated with it, which testify to the systematic spread of treponema.

TRM_HGEN.name: Gynecologic Hemorrhage
TRM_HGEN.description: Gynecologic hemorrhage represents excessive bleeding of the female reproductive system.

TRM_PLACENTA_PRAEVIA.name: Placenta Previa
TRM_PLACENTA_PRAEVIA.description: An abnormal implantation of the placenta at or near the internal opening of the uterine cervix so that it tends to precede the child at birth usually causing severe maternal hemorrhag.

TRM_HEMATOME_RETROPLACENTAIRE.name: Retroplacental Hematomas
TRM_HEMATOME_RETROPLACENTAIRE.description: Retroplacental hematomas are entirely behind the placenta and not touching the gestational sac.

TRM_MAP.name: Premature Delivery Threats
TRM_MAP.description: Preterm labor can result in premature birth.

TRM_PREECLAMPSIE.name: Preeclampsia
TRM_PREECLAMPSIE.description: A serious condition developing in late pregnancy that is characterized by a sudden rise in blood pressure, excessive weight gain, generalized edema, proteinuria, severe headache, and visual disturbances and that may result in eclampsia if untreated.

TRM_DEC_TROPHOB.name: Gestational Trophoblastic Detachment
TRM_DEC_TROPHOB.description: It's an early pregnancy bleeding refers to bleeding before 24 weeks of gestational age..

TRM_HDELIVRANCE.name: Postpartum Hemorrhage
TRM_HDELIVRANCE.description: Postpartum hemorrhage (PPH) is an obstetric emergency.PPH is classically defined by the volume of blood loss : estimated blood loss ≥500 mL after vaginal birth or ≥1000 mL after cesarean delivery.

TRM_PPCF.name: Postpartum Psychosis
TRM_PPCF.description: Postpartum Psychosis (PP) is a severe form of mental illness that occurs after having a baby.

TRM_HELLP_SYNDROME.name: Preeclampsia
TRM_HELLP_SYNDROME.description: A serious condition developing in late pregnancy that is characterized by a sudden rise in blood pressure, excessive weight gain, generalized edema, proteinuria, severe headache, and visual disturbances and that may result in eclampsia if untreated.

TRM_RUPTURE_UTERUS.name: Uterine Rupture
TRM_RUPTURE_UTERUS.description: Uterine rupture is a when the muscular wall of the uterus tears during pregnancy or childbirth.

SYM_CRP_BREAST_DISCH.name: Breast Discharge
SYM_CRP_BREAST_DISCH.description: Nipple discharge is the release of fluid from the nipple. It is a very common breast symptom and in most cases is part of the normal function of the breast rather than being caused by a problem.

SYM_CRP_BREAST_PAIN.name: Breast Pain
SYM_CRP_BREAST_PAIN.description: Tightness, tenderness or sharp pain in one or both breasts that occurs commonly and usually doesn't mean anything is seriously wrong.

SYM_CRP_SPEC_MARG.name: Spiculated Margins
SYM_CRP_SPEC_MARG.description: Spiculated margins are a true diagnostic feature of malignancy. Strands of tissue are seen radiating out from an ill-defined mass, producing a stellate appearance.

SYM_CRP_MASSE_HOMOGENE.name: Homogenous Mass
SYM_CRP_MASSE_HOMOGENE.description: Homogenous mass shown on an ultrasound image.

SYM_CRP_IRR_MNS.name: Irregular Menstruation
SYM_CRP_IRR_MNS.description: Missed, delayed or erratic periods or abnormal bleeding patterns. Irregular menstruation can have causes that aren't due to underlying disease. Examples include family history of irregular menstruation, menopause or menarche, physical stress, psychological stress, smoking or medication side effects.

SYM_CRP_DYSPAREUNIA.name: Dyspareunia (Female)
SYM_CRP_DYSPAREUNIA.description: Painful intercourse can occur for reasons that range from structural problems to psychological concerns. Many women have painful intercourse at some point in their lives. The medical term for painful intercourse is dyspareunia, defined as persistent or recurrent genital pain that occurs just before, during or after intercourse.

SYM_CRP_METRORRAGIE.name: Metrorrhagia
SYM_CRP_METRORRAGIE.description: Blood loss occurring outside menstrual periods.

SYM_CRP_AMENORRHEE.name: Amenorrhea
SYM_CRP_AMENORRHEE.description: Abnormal absence or suppression of menses.

SYM_CRP_SECHERESSE_VAGIN.name: Vaginal Dryness
SYM_CRP_SECHERESSE_VAGIN.description: Vaginal dryness is the result of decreased levels of estrogen.

SYM_CRP_LEUCORRHEE.name: Leukorrhea
SYM_CRP_LEUCORRHEE.description: A whitish viscid discharge from the vagina resulting from inflammation or congestion of the mucous membrane.

SYM_CRP_CONTRACTION_UTERINE.name: Contracted Pelvis
SYM_CRP_CONTRACTION_UTERINE.description: It is a pelvis in which one or more of its diameters is reduced so that it interferes with the normal mechanism of labour.

SYM_CRP_PREMATURE.name: Preterm
SYM_CRP_PREMATURE.description: Of, relating to, being, or brought forth by premature birth.

SYM_CRP_POST_PARTUM.name: Post-Partum
SYM_CRP_POST_PARTUM.description: Following childbirth or the birth of young.

SYM_CRP_ULCERATION_GENITAL.name: Genital Ulcer
SYM_CRP_ULCERATION_GENITAL.description: Genital ulcers, defined as single or multiple vesicular, ulcerative or erosive lesions of the genital tract, with or without inguinal lymphadenopathy, should lead to consideration of sexually transmitted infection.

SYM_CRP_SEBORRHEE.name: Seborrhea
SYM_CRP_SEBORRHEE.description: Abnormally increased secretion and discharge of sebum.

SYM_CRP_HIRSUTISME.name: Hirsutism
SYM_CRP_HIRSUTISME.description: Excessive body hair in women on parts of the body where hair is normally absent or minimal.

SYM_CRP_ACANTHOSIS_NIGRICANS.name: Acanthosis Nigricans
SYM_CRP_ACANTHOSIS_NIGRICANS.description: A skin condition that is marked by dark discoloration and velvety thickening of the skin especially of body folds or creases (as of the armpit, groin, or neck) and that is often associated with obesity, insulin resistance, hormonal irregularity, or medication use but may rarely occur as a sign of cancer (as of the stomach or liver).

SYM_CRP_BOUFFEE_VASOMOTRICE.name: Flushing
SYM_CRP_BOUFFEE_VASOMOTRICE.description: Flushing is to become markedly red in the face and often other areas of the skin, from various physiological conditions.

SYM_CRP_RH-.name: Anti-Erythrocyte Alloimmunization
SYM_CRP_RH-.description: Alloimmunization is defined as an immune response to foreign antigens after exposure to genetically different cells or tissues.

SYM_CRP_HYPERANDROGEN.name: Biological Hyperandrogenism
SYM_CRP_HYPERANDROGEN.description: High levels of androgens in females.

SYM_CRP_THROMBOCYTOSE.name: Thrombocytosis
SYM_CRP_THROMBOCYTOSE.description: Thrombocytosis is a condition in which there is an excessive number of platelets in the blood.

SYM_CRP_HEPATOMEGALY.name: Hepatomegaly
SYM_CRP_HEPATOMEGALY.description: Hepatomegaly is when the patient has an abnormally large liver. It's often a sign of a serious health concern.

SYM_CRP_HPV_HR.name: High-Risk HPV
SYM_CRP_HPV_HR.description: High-risk HPV can cause normal cells to become abnormal. These abnormal cells can lead to cancer over time. High-risk HPV most often affects cells in the cervix, but it can also cause cancer in the vagina, vulva, anus, penis, mouth, and throat.

SYM_CRP_ABSCESS.name: Abscess
SYM_CRP_ABSCESS.description: Buildup of pus within the tissue surrounding a surgical wound.

SYM_CRP_PERITONITE.name: Peritoneal Fluid Shows Infection
SYM_CRP_PERITONITE.description: Peritoneal fluid analysis has shown a result that is suggestive of an intraperitoneal infection.

SYM_CRP_SUPRACLAVICULAR_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_SUPRACLAVICULAR_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_AXILLARY_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_AXILLARY_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_NIGHT_URGE.name: Frequent Urination at Night
SYM_CRP_NIGHT_URGE.description: A frequent need to get up and go to the bathroom to urinate at night is called nocturia. It differs from enuresis, or bedwetting, in which the person does not arouse from sleep, but the bladder empties anyway. Nocturia is a common cause of sleep loss, especially among older adults.

SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE.name: Cyst
SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE.description: A closed sac having a distinct membrane and developing abnormally in a cavity or structure of the body.

SYM_OBGYN_HEMOPERITOINE.name: Hemoperitoneum
SYM_OBGYN_HEMOPERITOINE.description: Hemoperitoneum (sometimes also hematoperitoneum) is the presence of blood in the peritoneal cavity.

SYM_OBGYN_VEGETATION_KYSTE.name: Extracystic Vegetation
SYM_OBGYN_VEGETATION_KYSTE.description: Vegetation shown on an ultrasound image.

SYM_OBGYN_SYPHILIDE.name: Syphilide
SYM_OBGYN_SYPHILIDE.description: Skin lesion (with the exception of chancre) or mucous membrane of secondary and / or tertiary syphilis, presents the appearance of a spot, of a purulent rise or not.

SYM_SAC_EXTRA_UTERIN.name: Ectopic Bag
SYM_SAC_EXTRA_UTERIN.description: A fertilized egg lodges in a Fallopian tube or other location instead of continuing its journey to the uterus.

SYM_MPKO.name: Polycystic Ovary
SYM_MPKO.description: A polycystic ovary shown on an ultrasound image.

SYM_ATROPHIE_VULVE_VAGIN_COL.name: Atrophic Vaginitis
SYM_ATROPHIE_VULVE_VAGIN_COL.description: Atrophic vaginitis is inflammation of the vagina as a result of tissue thinning due to not enough estrogen.

SYM_KYSTE_ENDOM.name: Endometrial Cyst
SYM_KYSTE_ENDOM.description: A endometrial cyst shown on an ultrasound image.

SYM_MYOME_I.name: Fibrome
SYM_MYOME_I.description: A benign tumor consisting mainly of fibrous tissue.

SYM_MYOME_SM.name: Fibrome
SYM_MYOME_SM.description: A benign tumor consisting mainly of fibrous tissue.

SYM_MYOME_SS.name: Fibrome
SYM_MYOME_SS.description: A benign tumor consisting mainly of fibrous tissue.

SYM_KYSTE_OVAIRE_MULTILOCULAIRE.name: Ovaire
SYM_KYSTE_OVAIRE_MULTILOCULAIRE.description: A ovarian cyst shown on an ultrasound image.

SYM_KYSTE_OVAIRE_UNILOCULAIRE.name: Ovaire
SYM_KYSTE_OVAIRE_UNILOCULAIRE.description: A ovarian cyst shown on an ultrasound image.

SYM_TORSION.name: Ovarian Ascended
SYM_TORSION.description: Ovarian ascended on an ultrasound image.

SYM_ABCES.name: Abscess
SYM_ABCES.description: A localized collection of pus surrounded by inflamed tissue.

SYM_SORTIE_ORGANE.name: Prolapse
SYM_SORTIE_ORGANE.description: The falling down or slipping of a body part from its usual position or relations.

SYM_NEISSERIA_GONORRHOEAE2.name: Neisseria Gonorrhoeae
SYM_NEISSERIA_GONORRHOEAE2.description: Neisseria gonorrhoeae, also known as gonococcus (singular), or gonococci (plural) is a species of Gram-negative diplococci bacteria.

SYM_CHLAMYDIA_TRACHOMATIS.name: Chlamydia Trachomatis
SYM_CHLAMYDIA_TRACHOMATIS.description: Chlamydia trachomatis, commonly known as chlamydia, is a bacterium that causes chlamydia.

SYM_TREPONEMA_PALLIDUM.name: Treponema Pallidum
SYM_TREPONEMA_PALLIDUM.description: Treponema pallidum is a spirochaete bacterium with various subspecies that cause the diseases syphilis, bejel, and yaws. .

SYM_TRICHOMONAS_VAGINALIS.name: Trichomonas Vaginalis
SYM_TRICHOMONAS_VAGINALIS.description: Trichomonas vaginalis is an anaerobic, flagellated protozoan parasite and the causative agent of trichomoniasis.

SYM_CANDIDA_ALBICANS.name: Candida Albicans
SYM_CANDIDA_ALBICANS.description: Candida albicans (sometimes referred to as monilia) is a opportunistic fungus that is normally present on the skin and in mucous membranes such as the vagina, mouth, or rectum..

SYM_ATTEINTE_TUBAIRE.name: Tube Damage
SYM_ATTEINTE_TUBAIRE.description: Tube damage on an ultrasound image.

SYM_BHCG.name: B-HCG
SYM_BHCG.description: Beta human chorionic gonadotropin (HCG) is a hormone produced by the placenta during pregnancy, and is typically detected in the blood.

SYM_LIEC.name: Cervical Intraepithelial Lesions
SYM_LIEC.description: Cervical intraepithelial lesions at pap smear.

SYM_LEL_RU.name: Free Fluid in the Recto Uterine Pouch
SYM_LEL_RU.description: The cul-de-sac, also known as the pouch of Douglas or rectouterine pouch, is an extension of the postero-inferior reflection of the peritoneal fold between the uterus (anteriorly) and rectum (posteriorly). It is the most inferior aspect of the peritoneal cavity and therefore the first location where free fluid accumulates.

SYM_CYSTADENOCARCINOME.name: Cystadenocarcinoma
SYM_CYSTADENOCARCINOME.description: Cystadenocarcinoma is a malignant form of a cystadenoma and is a cancer derived from glandular epithelium, in which cystic accumulations of retained secretions are formed.

SYM_ADENOFIBROME.name: Adenofibroma
SYM_ADENOFIBROME.description: Adenoma with a large amount of fibrous tissue.

SYM_HYPERPLASIE_EP.name: Epithelial Hyperplasia
SYM_HYPERPLASIE_EP.description: Epithelial hyperplasia is benign proliferative breast lesion which arising as an unexpected new growth of epithelial cells.

SYM_CARCINOME_CANALAIRE.name: Ductal Carcinoma
SYM_CARCINOME_CANALAIRE.description: Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) is the presence of abnormal cells inside a milk duct in the breast. DCIS is considered the earliest form of breast cancer.

SYM_HTA_G.name: Gestational Hypertension
SYM_HTA_G.description: Gestational hypertension is defined as consistently elevation of systemic blood pressure above or equal to 140/90mmHg in a previously normotensive woman.

SYM_INSULINO_RPR.name: Insulin Resistance
SYM_INSULINO_RPR.description: Purpose of insulin resistance (IR) adapted by mother is to deliver enough quantity of nutrients to the growing fetus.

SYM_CLUE_CELLS.name: Clue Cells
SYM_CLUE_CELLS.description: Clue cells are epithelial cells of the vagina that get their distinctive stippled appearance by being covered with bacteria.

SYM_LOCHIE.name: Lochia
SYM_LOCHIE.description: The normal discharge from the uterus after childbirth.

SYM_ENGORGEMENT_M.name: Milk Stasis
SYM_ENGORGEMENT_M.description: Milk stasis is the buildup of milk within the breast tissue of lactating women.

SYM_LYMPHANGITE_M.name: Lymphangitis
SYM_LYMPHANGITE_M.description: Inflammation of the walls of the lymphatic vessels.

SYM_SIGNE_BUDIN.name: Budin's Sign
SYM_SIGNE_BUDIN.description: Budin's sign is positive if the milk is mixed with pus.

SYM_ABCES_M.name: Breast Abscess.
SYM_ABCES_M.description: A local accumulation of pus within the breast due to infection.

SYM_TVP_PP.name: DVT Detected
SYM_TVP_PP.description: Deep vein thrombosis detected with USG.

SYM_TVPELV.name: PVT Detected
SYM_TVPELV.description: Pelvic vein thrombosis detected with USG.

SYM_HYPOG_HYPOGT.name: Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism
SYM_HYPOG_HYPOGT.description: Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism (HH) is a form of hypogonadism that is due to a problem with the pituitary gland or hypothalamus.

SYM_IMAGE_COCARDE.name: Image in Roundel
SYM_IMAGE_COCARDE.description: Image in roundel with a central hyperechogenicity found on imagery.

SYM_IMAGE_DIVERTICULAIRE.name: Diverticular Image
SYM_IMAGE_DIVERTICULAIRE.description: "Mistletoe ball" or "Bilboquet ball" diverticular images found in imagery.

SYM_NECROSE_ISCHEMIQUE_HYPOPHYSE.name: Anterior Pituitary Ischemic Necrosis
SYM_NECROSE_ISCHEMIQUE_HYPOPHYSE.description: Intrasellar arachnoidocele with pituitary gland pressed against the sellar floor found on imaging.

SYM_ELARGISSEMENT_HYPOPHYSE.name: Pituitary Enlargement
SYM_ELARGISSEMENT_HYPOPHYSE.description: Symmetrical enlargement of the pituitary gland, thinning of the pituitary stem without affecting the sellar floor and clear and early hypersignal of the anterior lobe of the pituitary gland are found on imaging.

SYM_TSST1.name: TSST-1
SYM_TSST1.description: Toxic shock syndrome toxin 1 (TSST-1), a prototype superantigen secreted by a Staphylococcus aureus bacterium.

SYM_OBGYN_HYPERMOBILITE_URETRALE.name: Urethral Hypermobility
SYM_OBGYN_HYPERMOBILITE_URETRALE.description: Loss of tone of tissue in the proximal urethra.

SYM_OBGYN_ATTEINTE_TROMPES.name: Tubal Involvement
SYM_OBGYN_ATTEINTE_TROMPES.description: Inflammation of a fallopian tube on ultrasound.

SYM_OBGYN_LIG_TROMPES.name: Tubal Ligation
SYM_OBGYN_LIG_TROMPES.description: Patient asks to have fallopian tubes tied.

SYM_OBGYN_ABCES_BARTHOLIN.name: Bartholin's Abscess
SYM_OBGYN_ABCES_BARTHOLIN.description: The patient has an Bartholin abscess.

SYM_OBGYN_FISTULE_BARTHOLIN.name: Fistula
SYM_OBGYN_FISTULE_BARTHOLIN.description: Bartholinitis is fistulated on the skin.

SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_BARTHOLIN.name: Bartholin's Cyst
SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_BARTHOLIN.description: The patient has an Bartholin cyst.

SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_PILONIDAL.name: Pilonidal Cyst
SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_PILONIDAL.description: The patient has an pilonidal cyst.

SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_EPIDERMIQUE_VULVE.name: Epidermoid Cyst
SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_EPIDERMIQUE_VULVE.description: The patient has an epidermoid cyst.

SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_VULVE_INCLUSION.name: Inclusion Cyst
SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_VULVE_INCLUSION.description: The patient has an inclusion cyst.

SYM_OBGYN_HEMATOCOLPOS.name: Hematocolpos
SYM_OBGYN_HEMATOCOLPOS.description: A hematocolpos was found on imagery.

SYM_OBGYN_CHANCRE_SYPHILIS.name: Syphilitic Chancre
SYM_OBGYN_CHANCRE_SYPHILIS.description: The primary sore of syphilis, a painless indurated, eroded papule, occurring at the site of entry of the infection.

SYM_HEMORRAGIE_GEN.name: Gynecologic Hemorrhage
SYM_HEMORRAGIE_GEN.description: Gynecologic hemorrhage represents excessive bleeding of the female reproductive system.

SYM_PLACENTA_BAS.name: Low-Lying Placenta
SYM_PLACENTA_BAS.description: A low-lying placenta shown on an ultrasound image.

SYM_HEMATOME_RETROPLACENTAIRE.name: Retroplacental Hematomas
SYM_HEMATOME_RETROPLACENTAIRE.description: A retroplacental hematomas shown on an ultrasound image.

SYM_MAP.name: PROM
SYM_MAP.description: Premature rupture of membranes (PROM) is a rupture (breaking open) of the membranes (amniotic sac) before labor begins.

SYM_PRE_E.name: Placental Insufficiency
SYM_PRE_E.description: Placental insufficiency (also called placental dysfunction or uteroplacental vascular insufficiency) occurs when the placenta does not develop properly, or is damaged.

SYM_HEMATOME_DECIDUAL.name: Chorionic Hematoma
SYM_HEMATOME_DECIDUAL.description: Chorionic hematoma (also chorionic hemorrhage) is the pooling of blood (hematoma) between the chorion, a membrane surrounding the embryo, and the uterine wall.

SYM_ATONIE_UTERINE.name: Uterine Atony
SYM_ATONIE_UTERINE.description: Uterine atony is a loss of tone in the uterine musculature.

EXM_CRP_LIVER_FT_SAMPLING.name: Liver Function Test Sampling
EXM_CRP_LIVER_FT_SAMPLING.description: Liver function tests (also called LFTs) are blood tests that can provide information about how your liver is working. They test the levels of a number of proteins and enzymes that are either produced by liver cells or released into the blood when liver cells are damaged. \n\nRequired Lab: Haematology

EXM_CRP_HGPO_SAMPLING.name: OGTT Sampling
EXM_CRP_HGPO_SAMPLING.description: The glucose tolerance test, also known as the oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT), measures your body's response to sugar (glucose).

EXM_CRP_PELVIC_EXAM.name: Pelvic Exam
EXM_CRP_PELVIC_EXAM.description: A pelvic exam is a doctor's visual and physical examination of a woman's reproductive organs. During the exam, the doctor inspects the vagina, cervix, fallopian tubes, vulva, ovaries, and uterus.

EXM_CRP_SEIN.name: Breast Examination
EXM_CRP_SEIN.description: Visual inspection and palpation of breast.

EXM_CRP_X_RAY_MAMMOGRAM.name: Mammogram
EXM_CRP_X_RAY_MAMMOGRAM.description: A mammogram is an X-ray of breast tissue that can find changes too small to be felt during a physical examination. \n\nRequired Room: X-Ray

EXM_CRP_FCU_SAMPLING.name: Pap Smear Sampling
EXM_CRP_FCU_SAMPLING.description: A method for the early detection of cancer especially of the uterine cervix that involves the staining of exfoliated cells using a special technique which differentiates diseased tissue.

TRT_CRP_ESTRADIOL_CREAM.name: Estradiol Cream
TRT_CRP_ESTRADIOL_CREAM.description: Estradiol cream is a preparation of estrogen, a female hormone, for topical application to the vaginal area used to treat certain symptoms of menopause such as dryness, burning, and itching of the vaginal area and urgency or irritation with urination. \n\nRequired room: Any office.

TRT_CRP_ANTI_ACNEE.name: Anti-Acneic
TRT_CRP_ANTI_ACNEE.description: Medicine used to treat various types and forms of acne.

TRT_CRP_HORMONE_THERAPY.name: Hormone Therapy
TRT_CRP_HORMONE_THERAPY.description: Hormone therapy or hormonal therapy is the use of hormones in medical treatment. \n\nRequired room: Any office.

TRT_CRP_OP.name: Estradiol-Progesterone Capsule
TRT_CRP_OP.description: This medication contains 2 female hormones: an estrogen and a progestin.

TRT_CRP_ANTI_ANDROGEN.name: Anti-Androgens
TRT_CRP_ANTI_ANDROGEN.description: Antiandrogen agents block androgen receptors, thereby inhibiting the effects of male sex hormones.

TRT_CRP_PROGESTATIF.name: Progestogen
TRT_CRP_PROGESTATIF.description: A naturally occurring or synthetic progestational steroid.

TRT_CRP_OESTROGENOTHERAPIE.name: Estrogen Replacement Therapy
TRT_CRP_OESTROGENOTHERAPIE.description: Hormone replacement therapy involving the administration of estrogen without progestin.

TRT_CRP_INTRAVENOUS_IMMUNOGLOBULIN.name: IV Immunoglobulin
TRT_CRP_INTRAVENOUS_IMMUNOGLOBULIN.description: A purified blood product containing antibodies delivered by the intravenous (IV) route. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Orthopaedy dpt / Neurology dpt / DID

TRT_CRP_SURVEILLANCE.name: Medical Care
TRT_CRP_SURVEILLANCE.description: The provision of what is necessary for a person's health and well-being by a doctor, nurse, or other healthcare professional.

TRT_CRP_INCISION_AND_DRAINAGE.name: Incision and Drainage
TRT_CRP_INCISION_AND_DRAINAGE.description: Incision and drainage and clinical lancing are minor surgical procedures to release fuild or pressure built up under the skin. \n\nRequired room: Oncology Diagnostic Unit / Oncology Doctor's Office / ICU / Observation Room

TRT_PESSAIRE.name: Pessary
TRT_PESSAIRE.description: A device worn in the vagina to support the uterus, remedy a malposition, or prevent conception.

TRT_MYOLYSE.name: High-Intensity Focused Ultrasound
TRT_MYOLYSE.description: High-intensity focused ultrasound (HIFU) is a highly precise medical procedure used locally to heat and destroy diseased tissue through ablation.

TRT_OCYTOCINE.name: Oxytocin
TRT_OCYTOCINE.description: A hormone released by the pituitary gland that causes increased contraction of the uterus during labour and stimulates the ejection of milk into the ducts of the breasts.

TRT_ASPIRATION_ENDO_UTERINE.name: Vacuum Aspiration
TRT_ASPIRATION_ENDO_UTERINE.description: Vacuum or suction aspiration uses aspiration to remove uterine contents through the cervix.

TRT_OBGYN_REEDUCATION_PELV.name: Perineo-Sphincter Rehabilitation
TRT_OBGYN_REEDUCATION_PELV.description: Intravaginal manual work of the pelvic floor muscles, improves the control of urination.

TRT_OBGYN_CLOT_PREVENTING_DRUGS.name: Anticoagulants
TRT_OBGYN_CLOT_PREVENTING_DRUGS.description: Drugs preventing blood platelets to stick to each other and forming clots. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at Cardiology dpt

TRT_SALPINGOTOMIE.name: Salpingotomy
TRT_SALPINGOTOMIE.description: The operation of making an incision into a fallopian tube to remove an ectopic.

TRT_KYSTECTOMIE.name: Cystectomy
TRT_KYSTECTOMIE.description: The surgical excision of a cyst.

TRT_CESARIENNE.name: Cesarean Section
TRT_CESARIENNE.description: A surgical procedure involving incision of the walls of the abdomen and uterus for delivery of offspring.

TRT_OBGYN_ENDOSCOPIC_CAUTERIZATION.name: Endoscopic Cauterization
TRT_OBGYN_ENDOSCOPIC_CAUTERIZATION.description: Endoscopic cauterization is a surgical procedure where an electric current is used to burn/cautherize an area in order to open or close it off. It is commonly used to stop bleedings during surgeries.

TRT_EXERESE.name: Surgical Resection.
TRT_EXERESE.description: The surgical removal of part of an organ or structure..

TRT_HYSTERECTOMIE.name: Hysterectomy
TRT_HYSTERECTOMIE.description: A surgical operation to remove all or part of the uterus.

TRT_TUMORECTOMIE.name: Lumpectomy
TRT_TUMORECTOMIE.description: An operation to remove the cancer and some normal tissue around it, but not the breast itself.

TRT_OBGYN_LIG_TROMPES.name: Tubal Sterilization
TRT_OBGYN_LIG_TROMPES.description: Tubal ligation prevents an egg from traveling from the ovaries through the fallopian tubes and blocks sperm from traveling up the fallopian tubes to the egg.

TRT_OBGYN_MISE_PLAT_ABCES.name: Chirurgical Abscess Incision and Drainage
TRT_OBGYN_MISE_PLAT_ABCES.description: This consists of incising the vestibular mucosa vertically in the nymphohymeneal groove. One can also incise on the protruding dome of the gland. If the abscess is already fistulized, it is enough to widen the drainage hole sufficiently.

EXM_CBC_SAMPLING.name: CBC - Sampling
EXM_CBC_SAMPLING.description:
The sampling of blood for a Complete Blood Count (CBC) test. 

Required room: Hematology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Orthopaedy dpt / Neurology dpt / ICU / DID / Traumatology dpt 

Required equipment: Glassware + Hematoanalyzer

EXM_URGENT_BLOOD_ANALYSIS.name: PS Blood Control
EXM_URGENT_BLOOD_ANALYSIS.description:
Post surgery blood analysis is used in post-operative care in patients that could suffer from sepsis. 

Required room: Cardiography unit 

Required equipment: Lab analyzer

TRT_INCISION_AND_DRAINAGE.name: Incision and Drainage
TRT_INCISION_AND_DRAINAGE.description: Incision and drainage of an abscesses. \n\nRequired room: Any Diagnostic unit / ICU / Observation room / Doctor's office

TRT_ANTIPSYCHOTICS.name: Antipsychotics
TRT_ANTIPSYCHOTICS.description: Medication used in the treatment of various psychiatric disorders. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt / Neurology dpt

TRT_DIURETICS.name: Diuretics
TRT_DIURETICS.description: Medications that lower the amount of sodium and water in the body. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology / Neurology

TRT_CORTICOSTEROID_INJECTIONS.name: Corticosteroid Injections
TRT_CORTICOSTEROID_INJECTIONS.description: Synthetic steroid hormones is used to treat inflammation, and used on a broad spectrum of diseases. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Orthopaedy dpt
